name: paprika-flare

get_cookies.description: paprika-native Cloudflare bypass. Opens an ephemeral session on a paprika Worker, lets the Worker's real Chrome navigate to the URL (which auto-clears most CF JS challenges), optionally invokes page.agent() to click any visible 'I'm not a robot' / Turnstile widget, then snapshots the cookies via GET /sessions/{sid}/cookies. The cookies are IP-matched to the Worker that will run the eventual job (no IP mismatch unlike FlareSolverr running on Hub), and no extra Docker container is needed.

notes: First paprika-native plugin in the v2 Tool Registry. Unique value: (1) reuses the existing Worker fleet -- no separate container; (2) cookies are tied to the actual Worker's egress IP -- they will WORK for the subsequent job dispatched to the same NAT pool; (3) fallback to page.agent(engine='cogagent') for visual challenges (Turnstile checkbox). Trade-off: consumes one Worker lane briefly during the solve (~10-20s).
